key_insight: IA-NNP learns interface-aware Newton preconditioning to stabilize difficult cohesive-zone increments without changing the underlying traction–separation physics or final discrete solution set.
engineering_relevance: It can reduce step cuts, wrong-branch convergence, and solver stagnation in implicit fracture/delamination simulations for composite structures while preserving the original CZM response.
method: Acts only on active interface variables and leaves residuals, tangents, history updates, and dissipation checks unchanged.
Uses graph features from opening, traction, tangent, damage/history, mode mixity, residuals, and neighboring states to predict bounded corrections.
Provides two modes: IA-NNP-Init for initial-guess lifting and IA-NNP-NL for iteration-level nonlinear right preconditioning.
evidence_signal: Benchmarks on horizontal, circular, two-interface, and active-front cases showed better difficult-increment convergence, improved branch recovery, and fewer failures than standard Newton–Raphson and manual modification.
watch_item: Performance depends on reliable interface-state features and confidence gating; poorly trained corrections could still add overhead or fail on unseen fracture paths.
paper_title: Interface-Aware Neural Newton Preconditioning for Robust Cohesive Zone Model Simulations
